pattern: If the total gas covers the total cost a valid start exists, and it is found in one pass by restarting the candidate whenever the running tank dips below zero.
hints: A full loop is possible only when the sum of gas is at least the sum of cost; otherwise no start works.
Sweep once tracking the tank after leaving each station; whenever it goes negative, no station from the current candidate up to here can be the start, so the next station becomes the new candidate.

Keep a running total and a current segment total; if the current total drops below zero set start to the next index and reset the segment to zero; return start if the overall sum is non-negative, else -1.

edge_inputs.description: a unique valid start partway around
total cost exceeds total gas, impossible
single station that can fuel itself
start at zero works when surplus front-loaded
exactly balanced totals with a forced late start
all stations break even, start at zero

stress.description: construct a circuit with a guaranteed unique feasible start
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 5000))
    start = rng.randrange(n)
    gas = [0] * n
    cost = [0] * n
    # make every prefix-from-start strictly positive except returning to start exactly 0
    surplus = [0] * n
    total = 0
    for k in range(n):
        idx = (start + k) % n
        if k < n - 1:
            s = rng.randint(1, 50)
        else:
            s = -total  # close the loop to net zero
        surplus[idx] = s
        total += s
    for i in range(n):
        c = rng.randint(0, 50)
        g = c + surplus[i]
        if g < 0:
            c -= g
            g = 0
        gas[i] = g
        cost[i] = c
    return [gas, cost]
stress.note: running surplus from the chosen start stays positive until the loop closes at zero, so the start is unique
